content:
In addition to that I would bring attention to the moment that Phoenix’s interacts with the hunter. The threat is a perfect example of the racial discrimination that she encounters, and her reaction illustrates how familiarized she has become with the challenges regarding this matter. The toll that racism plays is both physical and emotional. It is obvious that the resilience she has is something that has come from years of enduring constant discrimination. The calmness she presents is powerful.

Phoenix shows her resourcefulness when she makes use of the umbrella as a cane and her ability to adapt and make the best of the situations she is faced with. When Phoenix repurposes objects, she shows that she has had to make a living with access to limited resources. Eudora Welty uses Phoenix to demonstrate how intertwined endurance and sacrifice are. As well as how much an individual is willing to go through for someone, they love despite challenges that arise.

from Week 5 Discussion

Nicole Cebulski
